title: 40th Anniversary of the Independent Software Industry
author: Peter S. Kastner
abstract: Kastner blog post on OnComputersTips marking the 40th anniversary of the independent software industry. On June 23, 1969, IBM unbundled software and services under US antitrust pressure, catalyzing the modern software business. Kastner recalls being a few months into his first programming job — combining COBOL labor-accounting programs for his employer, a subsidiary of Wang Laboratories — on an IBM 360-65 mainframe. Recounts the rise of Cullinet/John Cullinane, Computer Associates/Charles Wang, Gene Amdahl, and the 1970s-1980s minicomputer era.
importance_rationale: First-person Kastner memoir of the pivotal IBM 1969 unbundling decision — foundational moment for the independent software industry and Kastner's own career.
relevance_rationale: Serves as primary source for Kastner career bio (1969 Wang Labs employment) and for the Cullinet / Computer Associates / Amdahl origins of the software industry.
prescience_rationale: Memoir looking backward; no forward predictions made.
